untranslatable_reason: Horn Bottom requires precise multi-week candlestick pattern recognition with comparative spike heights measured across 3+ bars (two spike lows vs center week low). This pattern is inherently visual and geometric—comparing the exact relationship of three specific lows across non-contiguous bars and measuring spike magnitude from 'lowest low to the lower of two adjacent weeks' cannot be reliably expressed with standard bar-by-bar indicators. The pattern depends on identifying the 'inverted horn shape' and historical context ('no other spikes in preceding months come near this length'), which requires pattern-matching logic beyond individual bar conditions.
